one_time_description: Forgiveness of First Nation loan agreement - comprehensive land claim negotiation debt owed to Canada was forgiven effective March 31, 2020
economic_notes: Nation has investments in Homalco Holdings Limited Partnership (99.99%), Homalco Wildlife Tours Inc. (100%), Port Neville Logging Company (100%), and Homalco Resources Management Limited Partnership. COVID-19 noted as significant event in March 2020.
notes: Audit is qualified for two reasons: (1) no approved budget was prepared for FY2020 as required by PSAS, and (2) financial information for Homalco Holdings Limited Partnership, Homalco Wildlife Tours Inc., and Homalco Resources Management Limited Partnership was unaudited and ownership structure of Homalco Resources Management LP could not be verified. Major one-time item: $8,284,361 forgiveness of First Nation loan agreement (treaty negotiation debt). Nation went from accumulated deficit of $1,803,303 to accumulated surplus of $7,897,508 primarily due to debt forgiveness. COVID-19 noted as significant post-balance-sheet event. Contingent liability of $328,331 for ISC forensic audit recoveries under negotiation. Schedule of remuneration not included in the extracted text. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $2,968,240 + FNHA $921,040 + Province of BC $375,104 + DFO $223,996 + CMHC $8,191 + BC Treaty Commission $302,584 = $4,799,155. OSR = $7,063,702 - $4,799,155 = $2,264,547.
